untranslatable_reason: Signal requires monitoring divergence between multiple correlated time series pairs (8 pairs monitored with 50% threshold). This requires: (1) tracking correlation state across multiple external time series not in the allowed indicators list, (2) detecting directional agreement/disagreement across indexed pairs (requires array/pair indexing logic not supported by the 3-field condition schema), and (3) comparing divergence counts against thresholds. The allowed indicator set contains only single-series technical indicators and does not support multi-series correlation analysis, pair indexing, or divergence counting across monitored pairs.
translation_notes: This is a multi-series correlation signal (Hussman divergence logic) that fundamentally requires tracking relationships between 8+ external time series. The backtest engine's indicator set and condition schema are designed for single-series technical analysis and do not support the correlation tracking, pair indexing, and divergence aggregation this signal requires. To implement this signal, the engine would need: (1) multi-series correlation indicators, (2) array-based indexing for pair comparisons, and (3) aggregation operators to count divergence occurrences across pairs.
